---
title: "Lecture 13. Interval estimation and setting limits"
subtitle: "Statistical Data Analysis"
author: "Dmitry V. Naumov (JINR)"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: black
    width: 1024
    height: 768
    slide-number: true
    preview-links: auto
    chalkboard:
      buttons: false
    logo: img/dvnlogo.png
    css: styles.css
    transition: fade
    center: true
---

# Lecture 13

## Interval estimation and setting limits

---

## Draft Outline

- TODO: confidence intervals and coverage
- TODO: one-sided and two-sided intervals
- TODO: upper limits in counting experiments

---

## Exercises

::: {.callout-warning .exbox appearance="simple"}
## Exercise

Show that the likelihood for a Poisson count $n$ with expectation $\mu$ is
$$
L(\mu)=\frac{\mu^n e^{-\mu}}{n!}.
$$
:::
